ogTitle: Lower Mangrove vs Lower Peacock: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Lower Mangrove and Lower Peacock.
heading: Lower Mangrove vs Lower Peacock — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Lower Mangrove, New South Wales and Lower Peacock, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Lower Mangrove has a lower overall climate risk score (38/100) compared to Lower Peacock (41/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Lower Mangrove or Lower Peacock safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Lower Mangrove has a flood risk score of 51/100, while Lower Peacock scores 38/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Lower Mangrove scores 24/100 for bushfire risk. Lower Peacock scores 53/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Lower Mangrove or Lower Peacock?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Lower Mangrove has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.58% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
